news.headline: Caribou Biosciences CEO Highlights Off-the-Shelf CAR-T Progress at H.C. Wainwright Cell Therapy Conference
news.summary: President and CEO Rachel Haurwitz participated in a fireside chat at the H.C. Wainwright 4th Annual Cell Therapy Virtual Conference, discussing Caribou's progress toward commercializing its allogeneic CAR-T platform. She pointed to recent vispa-cel and CB-011 clinical data as evidence the off-the-shelf approach can match autologous CAR-T efficacy.

news.headline: Caribou Reports Dose Escalation Durability Data for CB-011 at EHA 2026
news.summary: A single dose of CB-011 produced deep, durable responses in heavily pretreated, BCMA-naive relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma patients, with an 83% complete response rate, 91% MRD negativity, and 50% of patients still in complete response at 15 months. Data were presented in an oral session at the EHA 2026 Annual Meeting in Stockholm.

news.headline: Caribou Reports Long-Term Vispa-cel (CB-010) Data in Second-Line Large B-Cell Lymphoma at EHA 2026
news.summary: Updated ANTLER Phase 1 data showed vispa-cel (formerly CB-010) achieved an 82% overall response rate, 67% complete response rate, and 17.1-month median progression-free survival in second-line LBCL patients. Caribou says the durability is on par with autologous CAR-T therapies, supporting its planned pivotal ANTLER-3 trial.

news.headline: Caribou Biosciences Presents at 2026 Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference
news.summary: CEO Rachel Haurwitz took part in a fireside chat at the Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference in New York, updating investors on the vispa-cel and CB-011 clinical programs. The appearance came ahead of key data readouts presented later that month at EHA.

news.headline: H.C. Wainwright Raises Caribou Biosciences Price Target to $11 After Q1 Results
news.summary: Analyst Robert Burns raised his price target on CRBU to $11 from $9 while maintaining a Buy rating, citing growing confidence in Caribou's allogeneic CAR-T pipeline following first-quarter results. The revision reflected encouraging early clinical trends across both the vispa-cel and CB-011 programs.

news.headline: Caribou Biosciences Reports First Quarter 2026 Results, Secures FDA Alignment on Pivotal ANTLER-3 Trial Design
news.summary: Caribou reported $118.6 million in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities as of March 31, 2026, and said it aligned with the FDA on the design of the pivotal ANTLER-3 Phase 3 trial for vispa-cel in second-line LBCL, enrolling roughly 250 patients. The company said its cash runway extends into the second half of 2027, funding CB-011 dose expansion and vispa-cel pivotal trial start-up activities.

catalysts.label: CaMMouflage Phase 1 dose-expansion data for CB-011
catalysts.detail: Initial dose-expansion data from the CaMMouflage Phase 1 trial of CB-011 in BCMA-naive and BCMA-exposed relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma patients is expected in the second half of 2026, building on the dose-escalation data presented at EHA.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings and business update
catalysts.detail: Caribou is expected to report second-quarter 2026 financial results and provide a pipeline update, likely including further detail on ANTLER-3 trial start-up progress; the exact date has not yet been officially announced by the company.

catalysts.label: Pivotal ANTLER-3 Phase 3 trial start-up for vispa-cel
catalysts.detail: Following FDA alignment on trial design (~250-patient randomized, controlled study in CD19-naive 2L LBCL), Caribou is funding start-up activities for the pivotal ANTLER-3 trial, with cash runway guided into 2H 2027; a formal trial initiation date has not been disclosed.

catalysts.label: Additional CB-010/CB-011 data at a major hematology conference (e.g., ASH)
catalysts.detail: Caribou has a pattern of presenting incremental follow-up data on vispa-cel and CB-011 at major medical conferences (ASH, ASCO, EHA, Tandem); further updates are plausible at ASH in December 2026, though not yet formally announced.

pulse: Caribou's two lead allogeneic CAR-T programs continue to post encouraging Phase 1 data: vispa-cel (CB-010) is showing durability on par with autologous CAR-T in second-line lymphoma, while CB-011 is delivering deep, durable responses in heavily pretreated myeloma patients, both highlighted at EHA 2026 in June. The company has FDA alignment on a pivotal ANTLER-3 trial for vispa-cel and cash guided into 2H 2027, and sell-side sentiment remains a Strong Buy consensus with rising price targets, even as the stock trades in the low single digits with the next catalyst being CB-011 dose-expansion data expected later in 2026.
